meaning: n.养家活口的人,负担家计的人
examples: In the past, women served as housewife while men were usually the breadwinner.
Families with modest incomes should insure the breadwinner or breadwinners first.
As the family increases in size, it is essential to add more insurance on the breadwinner to protect the dependents.
She tells me her husband is ill, she has six children and no breadwinner.
And the family model of a lone male breadwinner set a broad daily framework.
